text.title: php-eloquent Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for php-eloquent. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Always whitelist $fillable; never use $guarded = [] on any model.
rule.rationale: Guarded-empty models expose every attribute to mass assignment. Even fields like is_admin or password_hash can be set via $model->fill($request->all()) when $guarded is empty — a recurring CVE in agent-generated code.

rule.statement: Use Attribute::make() exclusively for accessors and mutators; never mix with legacy getXAttribute() on the same model.
rule.rationale: Mixing the two APIs on one model produces undefined behavior; the framework resolves accessor priority inconsistently across Laravel versions.

rule.statement: Eager-load in repository methods by default; never let controllers or resources trigger lazy associations.
rule.rationale: N+1 bugs caused by lazy loading in controllers are invisible in code review. Forcing eager loading at the repository boundary makes query cost explicit and reviewable.

rule.statement: Wrap multi-write paths in DB::transaction(); Eloquent does not auto-run transactions for create + relation->attach combinations.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit transaction, a failed attach after a successful create leaves an orphaned record with no rollback.

rule.statement: Disable lazy loading in non-production: Model::preventLazyLoading(!$this->app->isProduction()) — makes N+1 throw instead of silently slow.
rule.rationale: Prevents silent N+1 regressions that surface in production.

rule.statement: Always pair a model change with migration + factory + resource; agents that touch only the model cause drift visible in CI hours later.
rule.rationale: Keeps schema definition, serialization, and test fixtures in sync.

rule.statement: Use firstOrCreate / updateOrCreate over manual where-save patterns to avoid race conditions with concurrent writers.
rule.rationale: These methods handle upsert atomically within a transaction.

rule.statement: Resource classes are the API contract; never return raw models from controllers — leaked password_hash, remember_token, soft-delete columns.
rule.rationale: Decouples API shape from database schema.

rule.statement: Index every where/orderBy column in migrations; Eloquent makes it cheap to query unindexed columns but the bill arrives in production.
rule.rationale: Prevents O(n) full table scans on first production query.
